milestones.title: Audio Pipeline & Gapless
milestones.vision: Transform Musicode's audio playback from dispersed singletons (globalAudio, audioContext, analyserNode) into a centralized extensible audio graph with dual-element gapless playback and localStorage preference persistence. Zero user-visible regressions — the player sounds and works exactly as before, but the internals are ready for crossfade, EQ, and effects.
success_criteria: Player works identically to pre-M009: play, pause, seek, volume, queue, shuffle, repeat, visualizer, Media Session, scrobbling
Track transitions in album playback have no perceptible silence (gapless)
Volume, shuffle, and repeat settings survive page reload via localStorage
Audio graph has documented insertion points for future EQ and crossfade nodes

key_risks.risk: createMediaElementSource can only be called once per HTMLAudioElement
key_risks.whyItMatters: Dual-element approach requires creating both source nodes at graph init, not lazily. Incorrect lifecycle causes silent failures.

key_risks.risk: Browser differences in ended event timing vs actual audio output
key_risks.whyItMatters: Gapless swap precision depends on accurate end-of-track detection. May vary across Chrome/Edge/Firefox.

key_risks.risk: Tight coupling of usePlayer.ts to globalAudio singleton
key_risks.whyItMatters: Direct .src, .play(), .pause(), .currentTime, .volume access throughout — migration must be surgical to avoid regressions.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Two MediaElementSourceNodes from two HTMLAudioElements connected to the same AudioContext graph produce audio without conflicts

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: ended event timing precision for gapless swap
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Track transitions on FLAC files have no perceptible silence across Chrome and Firefox
All existing player features (play, pause, seek, next/prev, volume, queue, shuffle, repeat, visualizer, Media Session, scrobbling) work identically after migration

milestones.verification_contract: Unit tests for audioGraph module (graph init, volume, source switching), localStorage persistence (save/load/defaults/corruption), gapless timing logic
milestones.verification_integration: Full player flow in real browser with real FLAC files — play album, seek, volume, shuffle, repeat, visualizer, Media Session keys, scrobbling at 50% threshold
milestones.verification_uat: Play a full album end-to-end verifying gapless transitions, reload page verifying preference persistence, exercise all player controls verifying zero regression
definition_of_done: All slice deliverables complete — audioGraph module, localStorage persistence, gapless playback
Player has zero user-visible regressions vs pre-M009 (play, pause, seek, volume, queue, shuffle, repeat, visualizer, Media Session, scrobbling)
Track transitions in album playback have no perceptible silence
Volume and shuffle/repeat settings survive page reload
Audio graph exposes documented insertion points for EQ and crossfade nodes
Unit tests pass for graph logic, persistence, and gapless timing
Manual browser verification confirms all features work on real FLAC files
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: R017 (extensible audio graph), R018 (near-gapless), R019 (localStorage preferences), R020 (silent degradation)\nPartially covers: R005 (existing playback — must not regress)\nLeaves for later: R021 (crossfade, M010), R022 (EQ, M010), R023 (enhanced visualizer, M010)
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `audioGraph.ts` — singleton module exporting: `initGraph()`, `setVolume(v)`, `getVolume()`, `playSource(url)`, `pause()`, `resume()`, `seek(t)`, `getCurrentTime()`, `getDuration()`, `getAnalyserNode()`, `getInsertionPoint()`, `onEnded(callback)`
- `PlayerContext` refactored — useReducer actions delegate to audioGraph instead of globalAudio
- Volume controlled via GainNode (0.0–1.0 range)

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- Dual HTMLAudioElement architecture — two elements created at init, both connected via createMediaElementSource
- `audioGraph.ts` exports: `preloadSource(url)`, `swapToPreloaded()`, `getActiveElement()`, `getInactiveElement()`
- Source lifecycle management — which element is active, which is on standby

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- localStorage read/write utilities for audio preferences
- Initial state hydration pattern — PlayerContext reads from localStorage on mount

Consumes from S01:
- `audioGraph.getVolume()` / `audioGraph.setVolume(v)` for volume persistence
- PlayerContext refactored state (shuffle, repeat accessible for persistence)

milestones.title: Audio Experience: Crossfade, EQ & Visualizer
milestones.vision: Extender el pipeline de audio con funcionalidades audiophile opcionales — crossfade entre pistas, ecualizador de 5 bandas, y modos de visualización expandibles. Todo opt-in, desactivado por defecto.
success_criteria: User can enable crossfade (0-12s slider) and hear smooth overlap transitions between tracks
User can enable 5-band EQ, adjust sliders, and hear the audio change in real time
User can switch between 3 visualizer modes (bars, waveform, spectrogram) with animated panel expand/collapse
All audio features default to off — first play sounds identical to pre-M010 behavior
Gapless playback (M009) continues working when crossfade is disabled (duration=0)
All preferences (crossfade duration, EQ enabled/bands, visualizer mode) persist across page refresh via localStorage

key_risks.risk: Crossfade requires per-source gain nodes that restructure the existing audio graph
key_risks.whyItMatters: The graph topology changes from single-gain to dual-gain. If the wiring is wrong, all audio breaks — not just crossfade.

key_risks.risk: EQ bypass (disconnect/reconnect) during playback could cause audio glitches
key_risks.whyItMatters: Disconnecting nodes mid-playback on some browsers may produce clicks or momentary silence.

key_risks.risk: Spectrogram rendering is CPU-intensive with large FFT sizes
key_risks.whyItMatters: Could cause frame drops on lower-end hardware, degrading the player experience.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Crossfade overlap plays smoothly and gapless still works when crossfade=0
Toggling EQ on/off during playback produces no audible click or gap
Spectrogram renders at 60fps without visible frame drops on dev machine

milestones.verification_contract: TypeScript compiles clean (npm run build). No console errors during playback.
milestones.verification_integration: Audio graph nodes connect/disconnect correctly during crossfade, EQ toggle, and visualizer mode switch — verified via console logs and audible output.
milestones.verification_operational: N/A — frontend only, no server changes.
milestones.verification_uat: David listens to crossfade transitions, EQ changes, and visualizer modes. Confirms gapless regression-free.
definition_of_done: All three slices complete with UAT verification
Crossfade overlap audibly smooth at 3-5s duration
Gapless swap works when crossfade=0 (no regression from M009)
EQ modifies audio audibly when bands adjusted, flat EQ = no change
3 visualizer modes render correctly with animated panel transition
All preferences persist via localStorage across F5
No regressions in play/pause/skip/volume/mute/logout
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers: R020 (crossfade), R021 (EQ), R023 (visualizer modes). Partially covers: R022 (audio preferences — adds crossfade/EQ/visualizer prefs to existing volume/shuffle/repeat). Leaves for later: none.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `gainA`, `gainB` per-source GainNodes in audioGraph.ts
- `masterGain` GainNode (replaces current single `gainNode`)
- Graph topology: sourceA → gainA → masterGain → [insert point] → analyserNode → destination

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces:
- `analyserNode` (unchanged API, same getAnalyser() function)
- audioGraph.isInitialized() for render guards

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S02 (standalone)

Produces:
- 5 BiquadFilterNodes inserted between masterGain and analyserNode
- eqEnabled preference in localStorage
- EQ bypass function in audioGraph.ts

Consumes:
- masterGain and analyserNode from S01

### S03 (standalone)

Produces:
- 3 visualizer rendering modes (bars, waveform, spectrogram)
- Animated expand/collapse panel component
- visualizerMode preference in localStorage

Consumes:
- analyserNode from S01 (unchanged)

milestones.title: Quality & Hardening
milestones.vision: Blindar el aplicativo existente antes de añadir funcionalidad nueva. Mejorar mantenibilidad, accesibilidad, testabilidad y robustez del frontend y backend.
success_criteria: PlayerBar.tsx dividido en componentes <150 LOC cada uno, sin regresiones funcionales
React.memo aplicado en componentes de lista (TrackList, AlbumCard, ArtistCard) — re-renders medidos con Profiler
ARIA labels en todos los controles interactivos, alt text en imágenes, roles semánticos en landmarks
Tests unitarios para hooks críticos (usePlayer, useAudioAnalyser) y componentes extraídos — cobertura >60%
Servicios externos (Last.fm, ListenBrainz) distinguen timeout vs auth vs config errors en logs

key_risks.risk: Refactor PlayerBar puede romper audio pipeline
key_risks.whyItMatters: PlayerBar orquesta crossfade, EQ, visualizer y gapless playback — un error silencioso puede romper la reproducción sin que los tests lo detecten

key_risks.risk: Memoización prematura o incorrecta
key_risks.whyItMatters: useMemo/useCallback mal aplicados pueden causar bugs sutiles por closures stale o empeorar rendimiento si las deps están mal

key_risks.risk: Tests de audio hooks requieren mocking complejo
key_risks.whyItMatters: Web Audio API y HTMLAudioElement no están disponibles en jsdom — necesita mocks cuidadosos o test environment específico

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: App reproduce, crossfade, EQ y visualizer funcionan idéntico post-refactor — verificado en browser
React DevTools Profiler muestra reducción de re-renders sin regresiones funcionales

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Audio hooks no testables en jsdom
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Tests pasan con mocks de Web Audio API — cobertura >60% en hooks críticos

definition_of_done: Todos los slices completados y verificados
Build limpio sin warnings nuevos
Playwright E2E suite pasa sin regresiones
PlayerBar.tsx reducido a <150 LOC de orquestación

milestones.title: Structural Cleanup & CI
milestones.vision: Reduce complexity in the most critical frontend file (usePlayer), establish code-splitting patterns for sustainable bundle growth, and add automated test verification on every push via GitHub Actions.
success_criteria: usePlayer.ts reduced to ~120 LOC orchestrator composing dedicated hooks (useQueue, useScrobble, useMediaSession)
All pages except high-frequency ones (Library, AlbumDetail, PlayerBar) lazy-loaded with React.lazy + Suspense
ProtectedRoute enforces requiredRole prop on admin routes (/settings, /users)
GitHub Actions workflow runs mvn verify + vitest on every push and PR
All existing 345 tests remain green throughout

key_risks.risk: usePlayer refactor breaks playback behavior
key_risks.whyItMatters: usePlayer is the most critical file — controls all audio playback, queue, scrobble, and media session. Stale closures over refs are the #1 bug source in this hook.

key_risks.risk: Lazy routes break navigation or flash unstyled content
key_risks.whyItMatters: Suspense fallback needs to feel natural, not jarring. Route-level code splitting can interact with auth guards if not ordered correctly.

key_risks.risk: CI workflow flaky on GitHub runners
key_risks.whyItMatters: H2 file-mode tests and WireMock tests may behave differently on Linux runners vs Windows dev machine.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: All existing player tests pass + manual verification of play/pause/next/prev/seek/scrobble/media-session in browser
Navigation between all pages works, auth guard redirects correctly, admin routes reject non-admin role
CI workflow completes green on GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runner

definition_of_done: usePlayer.ts < 150 LOC, extracted hooks independently testable
Bundle produces multiple chunks (verify with vite build output)
Admin routes reject non-admin users with redirect
CI workflow triggers on push, both jobs pass
Zero test regressions (236 backend + 109 frontend)

milestones.title: Visual Experience
milestones.vision: Immersive Now Playing overlay with dynamic artwork-driven theming, enhanced visualizer integration, and scrobble status feedback. The overlay uses the existing dark theme by default, with optional dynamic color mode that extracts palette from album artwork.
success_criteria: Now Playing overlay opens from PlayerBar artwork click with slide-up animation
Overlay displays artwork, track info, controls, seek bar, and Up Next
Optional dynamic color mode extracts artwork palette and applies gradient background
Dynamic color preference persists in localStorage
Visualizer renders full-size inside overlay with all 3 existing modes
Track transitions animate artwork crossfade in overlay
Circular visualizer mode enhanced for overlay scale
Scrobble status indicator visible in PlayerBar using accent color
Scrobble errors surface via tooltip with accent/warning color
All existing PlayerBar functionality preserved (track name navigates to album)

key_risks.risk: Color extraction quality varies across artwork
key_risks.whyItMatters: Poor palette choices (too dark, too saturated, low contrast) make the overlay unusable. Canvas downscale to 64x64 + brightness clamping mitigates this, plus the feature is opt-in with dark theme fallback.

key_risks.risk: Visualizer performance at overlay scale
key_risks.whyItMatters: Full-size canvas rendering at higher resolution may drop frames on lower-end hardware. The existing requestAnimationFrame architecture handles this, but needs testing at larger canvas dimensions.

key_risks.risk: Overlay z-index and layout conflicts with PlayerBar
key_risks.whyItMatters: The overlay sits above content but PlayerBar stays visible. Getting the stacking context right without breaking existing layout requires careful CSS.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Canvas 64x64 downscale with brightness clamping 105-185 produces readable contrast for text over gradient background across varied artwork styles

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Overlay animation and layout works across viewports
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Slide-up animation from PlayerBar is smooth, overlay covers content area correctly, controls are functional, Escape/X closes cleanly

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Visualizer at full overlay size maintains 30+ fps
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: All 3 visualizer modes render at overlay canvas dimensions without frame drops on target hardware

milestones.verification_contract: Frontend tests pass (vitest --run). TypeScript compiles clean (tsc --noEmit).
milestones.verification_integration: Overlay interacts correctly with PlayerContext, AudioContext, and existing visualizer. Scrobble indicator reflects real scrobble state.
milestones.verification_operational: No performance degradation in playback. Canvas rendering at overlay scale maintains smooth framerate.
milestones.verification_uat: Manual verification: open overlay from artwork click, toggle dynamic colors, switch visualizer modes, change tracks, verify animations, check scrobble indicator states.
definition_of_done: Now Playing overlay fully functional with slide-up/down animations
Dynamic color mode toggleable and persisted, dark theme default
Visualizer integrated at full overlay scale with all modes
Track change animations (artwork crossfade) working
Scrobble status indicator visible with appropriate colors
All 109 frontend tests pass
No regressions in existing PlayerBar or playback functionality
milestones.requirement_coverage: R024 (Fullscreen Now Playing overlay) → S02. R025 (Visualizer color adaptation) → S01. R026 (Circular/radial visualization) → S03. Scrobble indicator → S04 (new requirement to be created).

milestones.title: Library Health Dashboard
milestones.vision: Panel que analiza la librería musical y muestra problemas de metadata (tracks sin artista, álbumes sin carátula, etc.) para que el usuario sepa qué corregir en herramientas externas como MusicBrainz Picard.
success_criteria: GET /api/library/health/summary devuelve conteos por tipo de issue
GET /api/library/health/issues?type=X devuelve lista paginada con ruta de archivo
Página /library/health muestra resumen con cards por tipo de problema
Click en card filtra tabla con tracks/álbumes afectados y su ruta
Todos los checks definidos están implementados y testeados
Panel incluye mensaje orientativo sobre MusicBrainz Picard

key_risks.risk: Queries SQL lentas en librerías grandes
key_risks.whyItMatters: Los checks combinados podrían hacer múltiples full table scans

key_risks.risk: Check de cover art requiere acceso al filesystem
key_risks.whyItMatters: No es solo SQL — hay que verificar existencia de archivo {albumId}.jpg

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Queries individuales ejecutan en <100ms con 900 tracks

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Check de cover art vs filesystem
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Album.hasCoverArt flag en DB es suficiente — no necesita leer disco

milestones.verification_contract: Todos los health checks devuelven resultados consistentes con el estado real de la librería
milestones.verification_integration: Endpoints REST devuelven JSON válido con paginación correcta
milestones.verification_operational: Queries ejecutan en tiempo razonable para librerías de ~1000 tracks
milestones.verification_uat: Usuario puede ver problemas de su librería y entender qué corregir
definition_of_done: Endpoints devuelven datos correctos para librería con issues conocidos
Tests unitarios y de integración cubren todos los checks
UI accesible desde sidebar o settings
Paginación server-side funcional
Verificación visual en browser

milestones.title: Synchronized Lyrics
milestones.vision: Display synchronized lyrics from LRCLIB.net alongside now-playing, with auto-scroll highlighting and DB caching. Plain lyrics fallback for tracks without timing data.
success_criteria: Lyrics fetched lazily from LRCLIB.net on first request per track and cached in DB
Synced lyrics display with current-line highlighting and smooth auto-scroll
Plain lyrics fallback when no synced version available
NOT_FOUND status cached to avoid repeated API hits, with manual retry option
Lyrics panel integrated into NowPlayingOverlay as toggle with split layout

key_risks.risk: LRCLIB coverage gaps for niche tracks
key_risks.whyItMatters: Users see 'not found' too often and lose trust in the feature. Mitigated by caching NOT_FOUND with retry option.

key_risks.risk: LRC timestamp sync drift
key_risks.whyItMatters: If parsed timestamps don't align with actual audio playback position, highlighting feels off. Need robust parser and tolerance margin.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: API returns synced lyrics for majority of vocal tracks in the library
Parser handles standard LRC, enhanced LRC, and edge cases without errors

definition_of_done: All backend endpoints tested (unit + integration)
LRC parser handles edge cases (multi-line, empty lines, malformed timestamps)
Frontend renders synced, plain, instrumental, and not-found states correctly
No regressions in existing NowPlaying functionality
Lyrics toggle persists across track changes

milestones.title: Waveform Progress Bar
milestones.vision: Replace the flat progress bar with a SoundCloud-style waveform showing amplitude structure of each track, enabling visual seeking through loud/quiet sections.
success_criteria: User sees a waveform visualization in the progress bar area for any played track
Played portion of waveform is visually distinct from unplayed portion
User can click/drag anywhere on the waveform to seek
Waveform generates lazily on first play and caches for instant display on subsequent plays
Flat progress bar shown as fallback while waveform loads or if ffmpeg unavailable

key_risks.risk: ffmpeg ProcessBuilder reliability across OS
key_risks.whyItMatters: ffmpeg must work on Windows dev (scoop install) and Docker Linux production — path resolution and process lifecycle differ

key_risks.risk: Canvas rendering performance during playback
key_risks.whyItMatters: Waveform redraws at ~60fps to update played/unplayed fill — must not cause jank on the audio thread

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Peak extraction runs correctly on Windows and produces valid JSON cache files
Waveform redraws smoothly during playback with no visible stutter

milestones.verification_contract: WaveformService unit tests, WaveformController integration tests, lrcParser-style unit tests for peak normalization
milestones.verification_integration: Real ffmpeg execution against test audio files, end-to-end waveform fetch from API
milestones.verification_operational: Waveform cache directory created on startup, graceful fallback when ffmpeg not found
milestones.verification_uat: Visual verification: waveform displays in PlayerBar and NowPlayingOverlay, seek works, played/unplayed colors distinct
definition_of_done: All slice deliverables complete
Waveform displays for real tracks in both PlayerBar and NowPlayingOverlay
Seek via waveform click/drag works correctly
Flat fallback shown while loading and when ffmpeg unavailable
All tests pass including new waveform tests
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `GET /api/waveforms/{trackId}` — returns `{ peaks: number[] }` (~200 normalized 0.0-1.0 floats)
- Waveform JSON cache at `./data/waveforms/{trackId}.json`

Consumes:
- Track entity with file path (existing)
- ffmpeg binary on PATH or configured via `musicode.ffmpeg-path`

milestones.title: Graceful Resize
milestones.vision: La app se adapta sin romperse al reducir la ventana hasta ~800px. Sidebar colapsa a iconos por debajo de 1024px, PlayerBar no desborda, overlays y popovers se ajustan. No es diseño mobile — sin hamburger menu, sin touch gestures, sin breakpoints portrait.
success_criteria: Sidebar colapsa a modo iconos automáticamente por debajo de 1024px de ancho de ventana
Sidebar permite toggle manual (expandir/colapsar) con estado persistido en localStorage
PlayerBar no desborda horizontalmente hasta 800px de ancho
TrackInfo en PlayerBar se adapta (oculta texto largo, reduce artwork si necesario)
NowPlayingOverlay usable a 800px — lyrics panel se oculta o apila verticalmente
Popovers (Crossfade, EQ, Volume) no se cortan por el viewport
TopBar search se adapta sin desborde
Sin regresiones visuales en resoluciones >= 1280px

key_risks.risk: Sidebar collapse afecta layout de todas las vistas
key_risks.whyItMatters: El contenido principal usa flex-1 relativo al sidebar — cambiar el ancho del sidebar impacta todo el layout

key_risks.risk: PlayerBar tiene 3 secciones con anchos fijos (w-60 + flex-1 + w-48)
key_risks.whyItMatters: A 800px con sidebar colapsado quedan ~740px para PlayerBar, las secciones fijas suman 432px mínimo

key_risks.risk: NowPlayingOverlay grid 1fr 1fr con lyrics no cabe a 800px
key_risks.whyItMatters: Dos columnas iguales a 800px dan 400px cada una, insuficiente para artwork + lyrics

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Sidebar colapsa/expande sin romper el contenido principal ni el PlayerBar
PlayerBar se adapta sin overflow horizontal hasta 800px
NowPlayingOverlay y popovers funcionan correctamente a 800px

milestones.verification_integration: Tests E2E Playwright existentes deben seguir pasando
milestones.verification_operational: N/A — milestone puramente frontend, sin cambios de backend
milestones.verification_uat: Verificación visual manual: resize de ventana a 800px, 1024px y 1440px comprobando cada componente
definition_of_done: Todos los slices completados y verificados
Verificación visual manual a 800px, 1024px y 1440px sin overflow ni elementos cortados
Tests E2E existentes siguen pasando
Sin regresiones en la experiencia desktop normal (>= 1280px)

milestones.title: Cassette Deck — Retro Mode
milestones.vision: A standalone full-screen cassette deck experience that replaces the standard player UI when activated. Physical tape physics (reel rotation, tape path, angular velocity), analog VU meters driven by the existing AnalyserNode, a detailed deck housing with odometer and transport LEDs, synthwave atmosphere (neon glow, scanlines, dynamic album colors), and an optional tape audio filter (lo-fi EQ, wow & flutter, hiss). This is the crown jewel of Musicode — the feature the entire project was built to reach.
success_criteria: Cassette deck renders full-screen as an isolated experience, activated via a dedicated button in the PlayerBar
Two reels rotate with physically accurate angular velocity — left empties, right fills, radius changes with progress, constant linear tape speed
Tape path visible between reels through guide rollers and head gap
Cassette label displays track title, artist, album with dynamic colors extracted from cover art
Dual analog VU meters (L/R) driven by AnalyserNode with realistic needle inertia (fast attack, slow decay)
Mechanical 4-digit odometer rolls with playback time
Deck housing with transport button indicators (PLAY/STOP/FF/REW LEDs)
Synthwave atmosphere: neon underglow, CRT scanlines, warm color palette, album-tinted accents
Optional tape audio filter: low-pass rolloff (~8-10kHz), wow & flutter via LFO pitch modulation, tape hiss
Play/pause/seek states reflected in reel animation (spin, decelerate with wobble, fast-forward acceleration)
Track change updates label and colors with crossfade transition
Exit returns to normal NowPlayingOverlay without state loss

key_risks.risk: Canvas 2D performance with complex scene (reels + tape + housing + VU meters + effects)
key_risks.whyItMatters: 60fps budget is ~16ms per frame. Complex path drawing, gradients, and shadow effects can blow this on mid-range GPUs. May need to optimize draw calls or simplify certain elements.

key_risks.risk: VU meter responsiveness and visual fidelity
key_risks.whyItMatters: The VU meters are the primary 'wow factor' — if they feel laggy, disconnected from the music, or visually cheap, the whole experience falls flat. Need careful smoothing and attack/decay tuning.

key_risks.risk: Tape audio filter introduces audible artifacts
key_risks.whyItMatters: Web Audio API parameter changes can cause clicks/pops. Wow & flutter LFO needs careful range to sound vintage without sounding broken. Must be seamless to toggle on/off mid-playback.

key_risks.risk: Canvas rendering complexity — diminishing returns on visual detail
key_risks.whyItMatters: It's tempting to add every detail (screws, chrome reflections, wear marks). Need to find the sweet spot where it looks gorgeous without becoming an unmaintainable rendering pipeline.

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Render cassette body + two animated reels + tape path at 60fps. If frame time exceeds 12ms, simplify before adding more elements.
Dual needle VU meters driven by AnalyserNode feel connected to the music with no perceptible lag. Attack < 50ms, decay ~300ms.
Tape filter toggles on/off without clicks. Wow & flutter sounds vintage, not broken. Hiss at correct level.

milestones.verification_contract: Canvas at 60fps. VU meters driven by real AnalyserNode data. Tape physics mathematically correct. Audio filter chain clean.
milestones.verification_integration: Existing player, visualizers, and NowPlayingOverlay unaffected. usePlayer and useAnalyser hooks shared without conflicts.
milestones.verification_operational: No memory leaks on repeated enter/exit. Canvas animation stops when not visible. Audio filter chain fully disconnected on exit.
milestones.verification_uat: Visual: cassette deck renders correctly, reels spin, VU meters bounce, atmosphere is synthwave. Audio: tape filter sounds vintage. UX: enter/exit without state loss.
definition_of_done: All canvas rendering at 60fps on mid-range hardware
VU meters react to actual audio signal in real-time
Tape physics mathematically correct (constant linear velocity, radius from tape volume)
Audio filter chain toggleable without glitches or clicks
No regressions in existing visualizer modes or player functionality
Works at all supported viewport sizes (desktop only, mobile out of scope)
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
### In Scope
- Full-screen cassette deck as isolated experience
- Canvas 2D rendering for cassette, reels, tape, deck housing
- VU meters with AnalyserNode integration
- Mechanical odometer
- Transport state LEDs
- Neon glow, scanlines, dynamic album colors
- Tape audio filter (lo-pass, wow/flutter, hiss)
- Dedicated activation button in PlayerBar
- Desktop viewports only

### Out of Scope
- Mobile/touch support
- Integration with existing visualizer toggle cycle
- Recording functionality (REC button is decorative)
- Multiple cassette skin themes
- Cassette flip (Side A/B) mechanics

milestones.title: UX Polish — Three Themes + Feature Upgrade
milestones.vision: Elevar Musicode a nivel de reproductor premium con tres themes intercambiables (Evolved, Novatouch, Minimal), home page con carruseles, play bar rediseñada, favoritos, queue/now playing panels, y glassmorphism. Inspirado en Astra Music pero manteniendo la identidad Musicode (multi-usuario, stats, scrobbling, cassette deck, activity feed).
success_criteria: Los 3 themes se seleccionan y persisten correctamente, cada uno con layout estructuralmente diferente
Home page muestra recently played, recent albums, recent artists con datos reales del backend
Play bar rediseñada con 3 secciones, waveform prominente, badges técnicos y controles de volumen numérico
Sistema de favoritos funcional (backend + frontend) con persistencia por usuario
Queue panel con drag-reorder y Now Playing panel con tabs INFO/LYRICS operativos
Glassmorphism aplicado consistentemente en cards y paneles
No regresiones en funcionalidad existente (playback, scrobbling, auth, cassette deck)

key_risks.risk: Theme architecture complexity — layout switching may break component assumptions
key_risks.whyItMatters: Si el ThemeProvider no maneja bien los layouts intercambiables, cada theme multiplica el trabajo de mantenimiento y debugging

key_risks.risk: Play bar redesign touches the most-used component
key_risks.whyItMatters: El play bar es el corazón de la app. Una regresión aquí afecta toda la experiencia de usuario

key_risks.risk: Backend changes (Favorite entity, new endpoints) may conflict with existing schema
key_risks.whyItMatters: Flyway migrations deben ser compatibles con la BD existente. Un error bloquea el arranque del servidor

key_risks.risk: Glassmorphism performance on lower-end browsers
key_risks.whyItMatters: backdrop-filter es costoso en GPU. Si no se aplica selectivamente puede degradar la experiencia

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Theme architecture handles 3 structurally different layouts
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: ThemeProvider switches between sidebar/horizontal/icon-only layouts without breaking shared components

proof_strategy.riskOrUnknown: Play bar redesign preserves all playback functionality
proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Audio playback, seek, volume, shuffle, repeat, scrobbling all work with the new 3-section layout

milestones.verification_contract: API contracts mantenidos — endpoints existentes no cambian firma
milestones.verification_integration: Backend tests pasan, Flyway migrations corren limpio, endpoints nuevos responden correctamente
milestones.verification_operational: Build de producción compila, Docker image se construye, app arranca sin errores
milestones.verification_uat: Browser verification de cada theme con playback completo, navegación entre vistas, y todas las features nuevas
definition_of_done: Los 3 themes renderizan correctamente y el selector persiste la preferencia

Tests backend pasan (existentes + nuevos para Favorite)
No regresiones visuales ni funcionales en features existentes
Build de producción compila sin errores

slices.title: Backend Foundation — Scan FLACs and Stream Audio
slices.goal: Prove the core technical bet: JAudioTagger reads FLAC metadata, Spring Boot stores it in H2, and streams audio with HTTP Range support. A FLAC plays in Chrome with seek.

slices.title: Browse API + Cover Art
slices.goal: Complete REST API for browsing the library: paginated tracks/albums/artists, album and artist detail, cover art serving, and global search.

slices.title: React UI Shell + Library Pages
slices.goal: Functional React frontend with dark theme, routing, API client, and all library browsing pages connected to the real backend API.

slices.title: Player + Queue — Full Playback Experience
slices.goal: Complete playback: click any track → audio plays in persistent player bar with seek, volume, next/prev, and queue. Playing an album queues all its tracks.

slices.title: Polish + Docker Compose
slices.goal: Production-ready polish: infinite scroll for large libraries, fix 'Unknown' artist in album tracks, loading/empty states, Docker Compose packaging.

slices.title: Fixes + Multi-format Scanner
slices.goal: Clean up M001 data issues and extend scanner to all common audio formats (MP3, OGG, M4A, WAV).

slices.title: Player Polish — Shuffle, Repeat, Keyboard, Navigation
slices.goal: Complete playback experience: shuffle, repeat, keyboard shortcuts, and click cover art in player bar to navigate to album.

slices.title: Test Suite Foundation
slices.goal: Establish test baseline: backend integration tests for API endpoints, frontend unit + reducer tests for player logic and utilities.

slices.title: Coverage enforcement — JaCoCo + Vitest thresholds at 80%
slices.goal: Enforce 80% line coverage on both stacks. Builds fail if coverage drops below threshold.

slices.title: User Model & Foundation
slices.goal: Establish the data model for multi-user auth — User and RefreshToken entities, repository, service, admin seed, UserDetailsService, PasswordEncoder

slices.title: JWT Auth with Secure Cookies
slices.goal: Implement JWT auth with secure cookies — login/refresh/logout endpoints, JWT filter, SecurityFilterChain locked down, CORS with credentials, existing tests adapted

slices.title: User Management & Role Enforcement
slices.goal: CRUD endpoints for user management (ADMIN only) and role-based access control on existing endpoints

slices.title: Frontend Auth Flow
slices.goal: Complete frontend authentication — login page, auth context, axios interceptor for cookie-based auth with transparent refresh, route guards, role-based UI visibility

slices.title: Caddy Reverse Proxy & HTTPS
slices.goal: Add Caddy as TLS-terminating reverse proxy in Docker Compose — single external entry point, HTTPS on localhost, Spring Boot internal only

slices.title: Java Modern Idioms
slices.goal: Modernize Java code — DTOs to Records where appropriate, var, Optional fluent patterns

slices.title: Backend Error Handling, Logging & Comments
slices.goal: Centralize backend error handling with @ControllerAdvice, add structured logging with MDC request IDs, add didactic comments to security code

slices.title: Frontend Error Handling, Logging & Comments
slices.goal: Add error resilience, diagnostic logging, and didactic comments to frontend

slices.title: Media Session API
slices.goal: Integrate Media Session API so OS media controls and keyboard media keys work with the player

slices.title: PWA Support
slices.goal: Make Musicode installable as PWA with app shell caching for instant load

slices.title: Spectrum Visualizer & Micro-animations
slices.goal: Add real-time audio spectrum visualizer and CSS micro-animations for visual polish

# S01: SpringDoc OpenAPI + Swagger UI

**SpringDoc OpenAPI integrated with Swagger UI, cookie auth, and full controller annotations**

## What Happened

Added springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui 2.8.14, configured OpenApiConfig with cookie-based auth, permitted Swagger paths in SecurityConfig, and annotated all 13 controllers with @Tag/@Operation/@ApiResponse. Swagger UI serves a living, auto-generated API reference at /swagger-ui/index.html. OpenAPI 3.1.0 spec available at /v3/api-docs with 31 documented paths. Pinned to 2.8.14 due to known incompatibility between 2.8.15+ and Spring Boot 3.4.4 (GitHub issue #3210).

## Verification

Swagger UI returns HTTP 200. /v3/api-docs returns valid OpenAPI 3.1.0 JSON with 31 paths. All controllers visible with descriptions and schemas. Commit ff663cf.

# S01: Service Unit Tests + Coverage Restoration

**Full unit test coverage for scrobble + activity + stats services; JaCoCo excludes dropped; bundle line coverage 87.10%**

## What Happened

Closed the coverage blind spot that had been hiding the scrobble/activity paths. Six tasks, all green. T01 added 13 StatsService tests. T02 added 16 LastfmService tests covering signature determinism against a hand-computed MD5 fixture, the mobile-session auth flow (success / no session / exception / blank config), and scrobble payload building (full/optional fields/null fallbacks). T03 added 6 ListenBrainzService tests covering the bearer token header shape, single-listen payload structure, and error branches. T04 added 14 ScrobbleService tests covering provider selection (none/only-LB/only-Lastfm/both/blank-token), retry on failure, retry on exception (fire-and-forget never bubbles), exhaustion after 3 attempts, and independent retries per provider. `BASE_DELAY_MS` was converted from `static final` to an instance field so tests can shrink it to 1ms — production default unchanged. T05 added 11 ActivityService tests covering subscribe/broadcast, recent-buffer cap at 20 with newest-first ordering, dead-emitter removal on IOException, and unknown-artist/album fallbacks. T06 dropped LastfmService/ListenBrainzService/ScrobbleService from the JaCoCo `<excludes>` block; `mvn clean verify` completed with BUILD SUCCESS, 168 tests passing, and 87.10% bundle line coverage (621/713). I/O-heavy services (LibraryScanService, MetadataService, CoverArtService, AudioStreamService) remain excluded.

## Verification

mvn clean verify → BUILD SUCCESS. 168 tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors). JaCoCo check: "All coverage checks have been met." Bundle line coverage: 87.10% (above the 80% threshold).

# S01: Service Unit Tests + Coverage Restoration — UAT

**Milestone:** M008
**Written:** 2026-04-16T18:04:05.880Z

## Objective
Verify that previously-untested services now have unit test coverage, the JaCoCo exclusions hiding scrobble services have been removed, and the bundle coverage gate still passes at ≥80%.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Each target service has a dedicated test class with passing tests

# S03: Flyway Migrations + Token Encryption

**Schema now lives in Flyway migrations (ddl-auto:validate) and scrobble tokens are AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest with tolerant read for legacy rows.**

## What Happened

S03 closes the schema-drift and plaintext-token risks flagged in the M008 roadmap.

**T01 — Flyway V1 baseline (D024).** Dumped the live H2 schema with `SCRIPT TO`, sanitized it into a portable CREATE-only SQL, and committed it as `V1__baseline.sql`. Per D024, the baseline is the real schema from previous milestones, not a JPA reconstruction — this guarantees we do not silently drift away from what the production DB actually looks like.

**T02 — Flyway wired + ddl-auto:validate.** Added `flyway-core`, configured `baseline-on-migrate=true` and `baseline-version=0` so existing databases pick up Flyway without replaying V1, and flipped `spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto` from `update` to `validate`. Test profile uses the same migrations — no drift between prod and test.

**T03 — Token encryption plumbing.** Added `TokenEncryptionService` (Spring `Encryptors.delux`, AES-256-GCM + PBKDF2) keyed from `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY`. Fails fast at startup if the key is missing outside the test profile. Added an `EncryptedStringConverter` JPA AttributeConverter that prepends a `v1:` version tag on write and tolerantly reads back anything without the prefix — critical for the legacy plaintext already in the DB. The converter is wired via a nested `@Component Initializer` that pushes the Spring-managed service into a static volatile field (Hibernate instantiates converters outside the Spring context).

**T04 — Apply + migrate + document.** Annotated `User.lastfmSessionKey` and `User.listenbrainzToken` with `@Convert`. Added `TokenMigrationRunner` (ApplicationRunner) that scans the `users` table via raw JDBC, finds rows not starting with `v1:`, and re-writes them as encrypted ciphertext. Idempotent by construction. Documented the key in `.env.example` and `SCROBBLING.md` including generation command and a rotation note.

No production behaviour regressions — existing scrobble flow and stats tests continue to pass. Coverage holds at 86% (gate is 80%).

## Verification

`mvn verify` in `musicode-server`: 187 tests / 0 failures / 0 errors / 0 skipped. JaCoCo instruction coverage 86% (gate 80%). App boots against existing `.mv.db` with Flyway logging `Successfully validated`. Starting against empty H2 applies V1 and seeds admin as expected. Wrong/missing encryption key reproduces fail-fast `IllegalStateException` at startup.

## Requirements Advanced

None.

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Operational Readiness

None.

## Deviations

None.

None.

## Follow-ups

"Key rotation story (re-encrypt v1: rows under a new key and bump the prefix to v2:) is deferred. BSM (Bouncy Castle / HSM-backed) is documented as a future option only."

## Files Created/Modified

- `musicode-server/src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__baseline.sql` — Flyway V1 baseline dumped from live H2 schema per D024.
- `musicode-server/pom.xml` — Added flyway-core dependency.
- `musicode-server/src/main/resources/application.yml` — Flyway config + ddl-auto:validate + musicode.encryption.token-key binding.
- `musicode-server/src/test/resources/application-test.yml` — Test profile Flyway + fixed encryption key for deterministic tests.
- `musicode-server/src/main/java/com/musicode/service/TokenEncryptionService.java` — AES-256-GCM text encryptor with fail-fast key validation.
- `musicode-server/src/main/java/com/musicode/util/EncryptedStringConverter.java` — JPA converter with v1: version prefix and tolerant read.
- `musicode-server/src/main/java/com/musicode/model/entity/User.java` — @Convert applied to lastfmSessionKey and listenbrainzToken.
- `musicode-server/src/main/java/com/musicode/config/TokenMigrationRunner.java` — One-shot idempotent re-encryption runner for legacy plaintext rows.
- `SCROBBLING.md` — Documented MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY generation, mandatory nature, rotation note.
- `.env.example` — Added MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY placeholder.
slices.full_uat_md:
# S03: Flyway Migrations + Token Encryption — UAT

**Milestone:** M008
**Written:** 2026-04-16T20:17:12.200Z

## UAT — S03: Flyway Migrations + Token Encryption

### Scenario 1: Boot against existing H2 with plaintext tokens
- **Given** an existing `musicode.mv.db` from before S03 with at least one user holding a plaintext `lastfm_session_key` or `listenbrainz_token`.
- **When** the app boots with `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` set.
- **Then** Flyway logs `Successfully validated` (no migration applied, baseline already recorded).
- **And** `TokenMigrationRunner` logs `re-encrypted N plaintext scrobble credential(s) at rest` where N matches the plaintext row count.
- **And** a SQL inspection of `users` shows those columns now start with `v1:`.
- **And** the scrobble endpoints still work (decrypt path transparent via JPA).

### Scenario 2: Boot against empty H2
- **Given** no `musicode.mv.db` on disk and `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` set.
- **When** the app boots.
- **Then** Flyway applies `V1__baseline.sql` and records it.
- **And** Hibernate's `ddl-auto: validate` passes (no drift between JPA model and V1 schema).
- **And** `AdminSeeder` creates the default admin as usual.

### Scenario 3: Missing encryption key outside test profile
- **Given** `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` unset and profile is not `test`.
- **When** the app starts.
- **Then** startup aborts with `IllegalStateException: MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY is required...`.
- **And** no partial context is left running.

### Scenario 4: Second boot after successful migration (idempotency)
- **Given** a DB where all token rows already start with `v1:`.
- **When** the app reboots.
- **Then** `TokenMigrationRunner` logs at DEBUG (`no plaintext rows found`) and performs zero UPDATEs.
- **And** decrypted token values still match the originals.

### Scenario 5: Tolerant read on mixed rows
- **Given** a DB with one plaintext row and one `v1:`-prefixed row in the same column.
- **When** a service reads both users.
- **Then** both return the original cleartext token to the caller via the converter.
- **And** after `TokenMigrationRunner` runs, both rows are `v1:`-prefixed at rest.
slices.goal: Replace ddl-auto:update with Flyway migrations and encrypt scrobble tokens (Last.fm session key, ListenBrainz token) at rest.
slices.success_criteria: App boots with Flyway-managed schema on existing H2 data (no data loss, no drift). ddl-auto set to 'validate'. Scrobble tokens stored encrypted (AES-GCM via Spring Security Encryptors.stronger). Plaintext tokens already in DB are re-encrypted on startup. Full test suite green; coverage gate (80%) holds.
slices.proof_level: Startup with real H2 file from previous milestones + `mvn verify` green + manual read of `.mv.db` confirms tokens no longer plaintext.
slices.integration_closure: Flyway migrations become the single source of truth for schema evolution. Token encryption is transparent via JPA AttributeConverter — service code does not change.
slices.observability_impact: Flyway logs each migration applied/skipped at INFO. TokenMigrationRunner logs how many rows it re-encrypted (no token content logged). Failures fail-fast at startup.

# S01: AudioGraph centralizado

**Consolidated scattered audio singletons into a centralized audioGraph.ts module with extensible Web Audio API pipeline**

## What Happened

Migrated the audio architecture from scattered singletons (`globalAudio` in usePlayer.ts, `audioContext`/`analyserNode` in useAudioAnalyser.ts) to a centralized `audioGraph.ts` module that owns the complete Web Audio API pipeline: `HTMLAudioElement → MediaElementSourceNode → GainNode → [insert chain] → AnalyserNode → destination`. Volume is now controlled exclusively via GainNode (element stays at 1.0). The insert chain slot between GainNode and AnalyserNode is prepared for M010 EQ/crossfade nodes. `usePlayer.ts` delegates all audio operations to audioGraph. `useAudioAnalyser.ts` reduced to a thin wrapper (marked for removal in M010). PlayerBar.tsx and Visualizer.tsx required no changes — the hook abstraction absorbed the migration. Added `audioGraph.stop()` for logout cleanup. Zero regressions confirmed by user manual testing.

## Verification

User manual browser verification: play FLAC tracks, pause/resume, seek, volume slider, next/prev, shuffle, repeat, visualizer toggle, Media Session keys, logout stops music. Console: zero errors, `[audioGraph] Graph initialized` log confirmed. TypeScript compiles clean. No imports of old `globalAudio` remain.

## Requirements Advanced

- R017 — Extensible audio graph pipeline implemented with insert chain slot between GainNode and AnalyserNode
- R020 — Graph init is resilient — safe to call multiple times, resumes suspended context

# S02: Persistencia de preferencias en localStorage

**Volume, shuffle, and repeatMode persist across page reloads via localStorage with graceful fallback to defaults**

## What Happened

Implemented during S01 execution as a natural extension of the audioGraph migration. Created audioPreferences.ts module that reads/writes player preferences to localStorage under a single 'musicode-prefs' JSON key. PlayerContext hydrates initialState from loadPreferences() and saves on every preference-changing action via useEffect. Invalid or corrupted localStorage data silently resets to sane defaults (volume 0.8, shuffle false, repeatMode 'off'). The implementation was delivered ahead of schedule because the audioGraph work naturally needed preference hydration for correct volume initialization.

## Verification

User manual browser verification: set volume ~0.24, shuffle on, repeat all → F5 → all values restored. Confirmed localStorage JSON matches. Clear localStorage → F5 → defaults applied, zero console errors. Volume slider and mute button remain functional after reload. Verified by user on 2026-04-18.

# S03: Gapless playback

**Near-gapless playback via dual HTMLAudioElement architecture — pre-load next track on inactive element, swap on ended event**

## What Happened

Implemented gapless playback using a dual-element approach: audioGraph.ts now manages two HTMLAudioElements (A and B) with independent MediaElementSourceNodes, both connected to the shared gain→analyser→destination chain. At ~3s before track end, the next track is pre-loaded on the inactive element. On the ended event, the active element swaps and playback continues immediately with near-zero gap. All edge cases handled: manual skip cancels pre-load, repeat-one restarts without swap, repeat-all wraps to queue start with gapless, repeat-off stops at queue end. Logout and stop clean up both elements. Volume, mute, and visualizer work correctly across swaps.

## Verification

User-verified 6 scenarios in browser: (1) gapless album transition — no audible gap, (2) manual skip — pre-load cancelled correctly, (3) repeat-one/all/off — all correct, (4) logout stops audio, (5) volume/mute persists across swaps, (6) visualizer stays connected after swap. Console logs confirm graph init, pre-load, swap, and cleanup.

# S01: Crossfade Transitions

**Dual-source audio graph with Web Audio gain ramps enables smooth crossfade transitions between tracks**

## What Happened

Refactored audioGraph.ts from single-source to dual-slot architecture (sourceA/sourceB → gainA/gainB → masterGain → analyser → destination). Implemented crossfade using linearRampToValueAtTime for sample-accurate gain transitions. Added crossfadeDuration preference with localStorage persistence. UI slider (0–12s) in PlayerBar popover with Blend icon. Fixed critical bug where track-load effect cancelled active crossfade — added isCrossfading() guard and immediate activeSlot flip. Edge cases handled: skip during crossfade, repeat-one bypass, short track guard, stop/logout cleanup.

## Verification

Build passes (npm run build). User-verified UAT: crossfade produces audible gradual fade between tracks, slider persists preference, icon reflects state, alignment matches other controls.

# S02: Memoización y rendimiento

**React.memo on 5 components, CurrentTrackContext for narrow subscriptions, stable callbacks in 3 pages — eliminates ~4 re-renders/sec across track lists and player controls**

## What Happened

Three layers of optimization applied: (1) React.memo on player sub-components (TrackInfo, TransportControls, VolumeControl) and AlbumCard — prevents re-renders when PlayerBar updates currentTime. (2) Extracted memo'd TrackRow from TrackList so only the active row re-renders on track change, not siblings. (3) Added CurrentTrackContext carrying only {trackId, isPlaying} with useMemo — TrackList, AlbumDetailPage, and Visualizer now subscribe to this narrow context instead of the full PlayerState, eliminating ~4 unnecessary re-renders/second from currentTime ticks. Also stabilized onPlay callbacks with useCallback and derived arrays with useMemo in AlbumDetailPage, TracksPage, and SearchPage. Caught and fixed a hooks-ordering bug where useMemo/useCallback were placed after early returns.

## Verification

TypeScript compiles cleanly. 21/21 Playwright E2E tests pass. Vite production build succeeds.

# S03: Accesibilidad (ARIA + semántica)

**Full keyboard navigation and screen reader support for all player controls, track lists, and popovers**

## What Happened

Four tasks delivered comprehensive accessibility improvements across 10 components. T01 replaced div-based click sliders in ProgressBar and VolumeControl with native `<input type="range">` elements — now keyboard-accessible with arrow keys, focusable with Tab, and announcing values to screen readers via aria-valuetext. T02 made TrackRow keyboard-activatable (role="button", tabIndex, Enter/Space handler, aria-current for playing track) and added aria-label + aria-pressed to all TransportControls buttons and Visualizer mode selector. T03 gave both popovers (Crossfade, EQ) full dialog accessibility: aria-haspopup/aria-expanded on triggers, role="dialog" on containers, Escape key to close, focus auto-moves into dialog on open and returns to trigger on close. EQ toggle converted to role="switch" with aria-checked. All band sliders labeled. T04 wrapped PlayerBar in role="region" aria-label="Music player", hid decorative vinyl animation from screen reader, and added descriptive aria-labels to album links in TrackInfo and AlbumCard.

# S05: Error handling servicios externos + cleanup

**Typed error classification for scrobble services, smart retry (no-retry on auth), and AbortController cleanup on frontend unmount**

## What Happened

Backend: Replaced boolean returns in LastfmService.scrobble() and ListenBrainzService.submitListen() with ScrobbleResult record carrying success/errorType/message. Typed catch blocks distinguish HttpClientErrorException (401/403→AUTH_ERROR), HttpServerErrorException (5xx→SERVER_ERROR), ResourceAccessException (timeout→TIMEOUT), config checks (→CONFIG_ERROR), and fallback (→UNKNOWN). Log markers now differentiate: LASTFM_AUTH_ERROR, LASTFM_TIMEOUT, LB_SERVER_ERROR, etc. ScrobbleService uses ScrobbleResult.isRetryable() — AUTH_ERROR and CONFIG_ERROR bail immediately (1 call), TIMEOUT and SERVER_ERROR retry with exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s). Frontend: Added AbortController to AuthContext.getMe() on mount and usePlayer.recordPlay() — both abort in-flight requests on unmount. Fixed tsconfig.app.json to exclude test files from the production build.

## Verification

Backend: mvn test — 236 tests pass (0 failures). WireMock tests assert correct ErrorType for 401, 503, connection reset, 429. ScrobbleServiceTest verifies no-retry for AUTH_ERROR/CONFIG_ERROR, retry for TIMEOUT/SERVER_ERROR. Frontend: tsc --noEmit clean, npm run build succeeds, vitest 109/109 pass.

# S01: Extract usePlayer into composable hooks

**Split usePlayer.ts (418 LOC) into 4 focused hooks: usePlayer orchestrator (190), useScrobble (45), useMediaSession (102), useGapless (155)**

## What Happened

Extracted three self-contained concerns from usePlayer.ts into dedicated hooks:

1. **useScrobble** (45 LOC) — play reporting at 50% threshold with AbortController cleanup. Pure side-effect hook, no return value.

2. **useMediaSession** (102 LOC) — all navigator.mediaSession logic: metadata sync on track change, playback state sync, action handler registration (owner-gated), and position state updates for OS seek bars.

3. **useGapless** (155 LOC) — the most complex extraction. Owns audioGraph event wiring (onTimeUpdate, onLoadedMetadata, onEnded), gapless preload triggering, crossfade triggering, and the swap-on-ended logic. Maintains its own refs for queue/index/repeatMode/currentTrack to avoid stale closures. Returns setCrossfadeDuration/getCrossfadeDuration.

**usePlayer** (190 LOC) is now a thin orchestrator: claims ownership, composes the 3 hooks, syncs volume/source/play-pause to audioGraph, and exposes the unchanged public API.

Key decision: changed ownership tracking from useRef to useState. The original ref worked because ownership claim and event wiring happened in the same useEffect. After extraction, child hooks receive isOwner as a prop — a ref mutation wouldn't trigger re-render, so useGapless would never see isOwner=true. useState fixes this cleanly.

Zero consumer changes — all 5 files importing usePlayer (AppShell, PlayerBar, SearchPage, AlbumDetailPage, TracksPage) work unchanged.

## Verification

vitest --run: 109/109 tests pass. tsc --noEmit: zero errors. usePlayer.ts: 190 LOC (down from 418, 55% reduction). No import changes in any consumer.

1. Toggle shuffle → queue randomizes, current track stays
2. Toggle repeat modes (off → all → one)
3. Verify end-of-queue behavior for each mode
- **Pass criteria:** Same behavior as pre-refactor

# S02: Lazy routes and role-based guards

**Admin routes guarded with requiredRole='ADMIN', LoginPage lazy-loaded — all pages except AlbumsPage now code-split**

## What Happened

The ProtectedRoute component already supported requiredRole but it wasn't wired to admin routes. Moved /settings and /users into a separate ProtectedRoute group with requiredRole='ADMIN'. Non-admin users get redirected to / if they navigate directly. The sidebar already hid admin links behind isAdmin, so the UX was already correct — this adds the route-level enforcement.

LoginPage made lazy to complete code-splitting. AlbumsPage stays eager as the landing page — it's what users see first, so it should load immediately.

# S01: Color extraction engine with opt-in dynamic theming

**Canvas-based color extraction from album artwork with opt-in CSS variable injection, cached by albumId**

## What Happened

Built the foundation for dynamic theming: a color extraction engine that downscales album artwork to 64x64 canvas, quantizes pixels into frequency buckets, picks top 2 dominant colors, and normalizes brightness to 105-185 range for readability. Results cached in memory by albumId. The useDynamicTheme hook connects extraction to PlayerContext — when enabled, it sets CSS custom properties (--np-color-1, --np-color-2, --np-bg) on document root on track change. Disabled by default; toggle persists via audioPreferences localStorage. Fallback palette uses existing indigo/zinc theme colors.

## Verification

TypeScript clean, 117 tests pass (8 new: 5 colorExtraction + 3 audioPreferences dynamicTheme)

## Requirements Advanced

- R025 — Color extraction engine implemented and ready for consumers (Now Playing overlay in S02/S03)

# S01: Backend: Health Service + API endpoints

**LibraryHealthService with 8 health checks, paginated REST endpoints, full test coverage**

## What Happened

Implemented the complete backend for library health analysis. LibraryHealthService runs 8 checks against the music library: missing title, missing artist, missing album, missing track number, missing year, missing genre, albums without cover art, and albums with inconsistent artists. Each check returns counts and paginated detail lists. Two REST endpoints expose the data: /api/library/health/summary for aggregated counts and /api/library/health/issues?type=X for filtered paginated results. Custom JPQL queries in TrackRepository and AlbumRepository handle the complex checks efficiently. Full integration test coverage validates all checks with positive/negative cases and edge cases.

## Verification

mvn -B verify: 272 tests, 0 failures. Manual curl to /api/library/health/summary returns correct counts. Paginated issues endpoint returns expected results with proper page metadata.

# S01: Collapsible Sidebar

**Sidebar collapses to icon-only mode automatically below 1024px and via manual toggle, with localStorage persistence and smooth transitions.**

## What Happened

Implemented a collapsible sidebar with three behaviors: auto-collapse below 1024px viewport width, auto-expand above 1024px (unless manually overridden), and manual toggle via a button. The `useSidebarCollapse` hook manages state with localStorage persistence and a `manualOverride` flag that prevents auto-expand from fighting the user's intent. Sidebar transitions use CSS width animation (w-64 ↔ w-16) with opacity transitions on labels. Collapsed mode shows centered icons with native title tooltips. The toggle button uses PanelLeft/PanelLeftClose icons for clear affordance.

## Verification

Manual browser verification: resize below 1024px → auto-collapse confirmed, resize above 1024px → auto-expand confirmed, manual toggle works in both directions, localStorage persists state across reload.

# S01: Theme Architecture + Evolved Shell

**Built complete CSS variable theming system with 3 switchable layout shells (Evolved/Novatouch/Minimal) and migrated all components to CSS custom properties**

## What Happened

S01 delivered the full theming foundation for Musicode in 4 tasks:

**T01** — Created ThemeProvider context, TypeScript types (ThemeName, ShellLayout, ThemeTokens with 40+ tokens, ThemeConfig), useTheme hook, and token sets for all 3 themes. ThemeProvider injects --mc-* CSS variables on :root, persists to localStorage, and sets data-theme attribute.

**T02** — Refactored AppShell into a theme-aware shell architecture. Created EvolvedShell (sidebar + topbar), NovatouchShell (56px icon sidebar), and MinimalShell (horizontal nav). AppShell delegates to the active shell based on ThemeContext. Keyboard shortcuts shared across all shells.

**T03** — Migrated ~40 component files from hardcoded Tailwind zinc/indigo classes to CSS variable equivalents using arbitrary value syntax (bg-[var(--mc-bgBase)]). Added glassmorphism utilities to index.css. Visual no-op — Evolved theme renders identically to pre-migration.

**T04** — Built ThemeSelector segmented control. Iterated on placement through user testing: started in TopBar, tried popover in Novatouch sidebar, ultimately settled in Settings page (Appearance section) as the canonical home. Unified accent colors to indigo across all 3 themes per user feedback.

All subsequent slices (S02-S08) build on this foundation — every component now consumes CSS variables that the theme system controls.

## Verification

TypeScript compiles clean (tsc --noEmit). Vite production build passes. All 3 themes verified in browser: layout shells switch correctly, CSS variables apply, accent colors are consistent indigo, localStorage persists theme choice across reload. User confirmed all themes working correctly and approved closure.

## Requirements Advanced

None.

## Requirements Validated

None.

## New Requirements Surfaced

None.

## Requirements Invalidated or Re-scoped

None.

## Operational Readiness

None.

## Deviations

T03 expanded from 7 planned files to ~40 files to ensure complete migration. T04 iterated on theme selector placement (TopBar → popover → Settings page) based on user testing.

## Known Limitations

None.

## Follow-ups

S02 (Home Page) is next in the roadmap. S07/S08 will flesh out Novatouch and Minimal shells beyond the current placeholders.

## Files Created/Modified

None.

# T01: Added SpringDoc OpenAPI dependency, Security config, and OpenApiConfig bean with cookie auth

## What Happened

Added springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui 2.8.14 to pom.xml (pinned due to 2.8.15+ incompatibility with Spring Boot 3.4.4). Created OpenApiConfig with app title, version, cookie-based auth scheme. | 2 | `curl -s http://localhost:8080/v3/api-docs | jq .openapi` | 0 | pass | 150ms |

## Deviations

None.

## Known Issues

None.

# T04: ScrobbleService retry tests: 14 tests covering provider selection, backoff retries, fire-and-forget

**ScrobbleService retry tests: 14 tests covering provider selection, backoff retries, fire-and-forget**

## What Happened

Wrote ScrobbleServiceTest with 14 cases. To avoid multi-second sleeps from the exponential backoff (1s→2s→4s), changed `BASE_DELAY_MS` from a `static final` constant to a `baseDelayMs` instance field so tests can override it to 1ms via ReflectionTestUtils — this is the least-invasive refactor and keeps production behavior identical. Coverage: provider selection (none/only LB/only Lastfm/both/blank-string tokens), track-not-found short-circuit, success on first try (no retry), success after 1 or 2 retries (stops on success), exhaustion after 3 attempts when always false, exception path (fire-and-forget — never bubbles up), independent retries per provider, and verification that the reloaded track from the repository is the one passed to providers (not the stub on the event).

## Verification

`mvn -Dtest=ScrobbleServiceTest test` → 14 passed in 1.1s.

Wrote ActivityServiceTest with 11 cases. Coverage: subscribe returns live emitter + count increments; multiple clients tracked independently; broadcast populates the recent buffer newest-first; buffer caps at 20 with oldest evicted (verified with 25 broadcasts); unknown-artist/album fallbacks; broadcast calls send() on all active emitters (mocks injected via ReflectionTestUtils); emitters that throw IOException during send are removed from the active list; broadcast with zero emitters still records the event in recent; getRecent() returns an unmodifiable copy; fresh service has zero connections. tasks.title: Flyway V1 baseline created from real H2 dump; DDL matches live DB byte-for-byte.
tasks.one_liner: Flyway V1 baseline created from real H2 dump; DDL matches live DB byte-for-byte.
tasks.narrative:
Stopped the running Spring Boot server to release the H2 file lock (taskkill on PID 30124 — wmic isn't installed, tasklist/taskkill substituted). Exported the live schema + data with `org.h2.tools.Script` against `jdbc:h2:file:./data/musicode-db;IFEXISTS=TRUE` into `/tmp/h2dump/full-dump.sql` (129 lines, 7 tables, 1 user, 17 tracks, 8 refresh tokens, etc.). Sanitized it down to a schema-only artifact at `src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__baseline.sql`.

Cleanup applied to the raw dump (documented in-file):

1. Removed `CREATE USER "SA" SALT ... HASH ... ADMIN` — environment-specific credential, not schema. H2 creates SA automatically when the JDBC user matches.
2. Removed `SET DB_CLOSE_DELAY -1` — session-level knob, already set via JDBC URL in application.yml.
3. Removed all `INSERT` statements and `-- N +/- SELECT COUNT(*)` comments — this is a baseline for schema, not data. Existing rows stay in place because Flyway will use `baseline-on-migrate=true` in T02.
4. Changed `CREATE CACHED TABLE` to `CREATE TABLE` — `CACHED` is a legacy H2 storage hint (tables are CACHED by default in 2.x). Keeping it portable for any future H2 / alternative engine.
5. Stripped `NOCHECK` from foreign keys — NOCHECK is a restore-time flag used by SCRIPT TO to skip validation during reload; real migrations need validation ON.

Kept as-is (important for Flyway validation against existing DBs): the H2-generated constraint names (`CONSTRAINT_7`, `CONSTRAINT_F`, `UKR43AF9AP4EDM43MMTQ01ODDJ6`, etc.), the two explicit indexes on PLAYBACK_EVENTS, the identity-column `RESTART WITH N` values, and `NULLS DISTINCT`/`NULLS FIRST` clauses. These match the live `.mv.db` exactly — which is the whole point of D024.
tasks.verification_result: Applied V1 to a fresh H2 via `org.h2.tools.RunScript` (exit 0), then re-dumped the resulting DB with `org.h2.tools.Script` and diffed against the live dump. After stripping runtime-only lines (CREATE USER, SET DB_CLOSE_DELAY, INSERTs, row-count comments) and normalizing whitespace, the two DDL statement sets are identical: 28 statements on each side, `diff` exits 0.
tasks.known_issues: Server was stopped to release the H2 file lock; not restarted yet (T02 will wire Flyway and we'll start fresh). The `data/musicode-db.mv.db` file is intact and untouched by this task.
key_decisions: Honored D024 — baseline is the real on-disk schema, not a JPA reconstruction. Constraint and index names are preserved so Flyway's baseline-on-migrate won't drift against the existing .mv.db.
Dropped environment-specific lines (CREATE USER hash, SET DB_CLOSE_DELAY) and restore-time flags (NOCHECK) — these belong to a dump, not a migration.

# T01: Flyway V1 baseline created from real H2 dump; DDL matches live DB byte-for-byte.

**Flyway V1 baseline created from real H2 dump; DDL matches live DB byte-for-byte.**

## What Happened

Stopped the running Spring Boot server to release the H2 file lock (taskkill on PID 30124 — wmic isn't installed, tasklist/taskkill substituted). Exported the live schema + data with `org.h2.tools.Script` against `jdbc:h2:file:./data/musicode-db;IFEXISTS=TRUE` into `/tmp/h2dump/full-dump.sql` (129 lines, 7 tables, 1 user, 17 tracks, 8 refresh tokens, etc.). Sanitized it down to a schema-only artifact at `src/main/resources/db/migration/V1__baseline.sql`.

Cleanup applied to the raw dump (documented in-file):

1. Removed `CREATE USER "SA" SALT ... HASH ... ADMIN` — environment-specific credential, not schema. H2 creates SA automatically when the JDBC user matches.
2. Removed `SET DB_CLOSE_DELAY -1` — session-level knob, already set via JDBC URL in application.yml.
3. Removed all `INSERT` statements and `-- N +/- SELECT COUNT(*)` comments — this is a baseline for schema, not data. Existing rows stay in place because Flyway will use `baseline-on-migrate=true` in T02.
4. Changed `CREATE CACHED TABLE` to `CREATE TABLE` — `CACHED` is a legacy H2 storage hint (tables are CACHED by default in 2.x). Keeping it portable for any future H2 / alternative engine.
5. Stripped `NOCHECK` from foreign keys — NOCHECK is a restore-time flag used by SCRIPT TO to skip validation during reload; real migrations need validation ON.

Kept as-is (important for Flyway validation against existing DBs): the H2-generated constraint names (`CONSTRAINT_7`, `CONSTRAINT_F`, `UKR43AF9AP4EDM43MMTQ01ODDJ6`, etc.), the two explicit indexes on PLAYBACK_EVENTS, the identity-column `RESTART WITH N` values, and `NULLS DISTINCT`/`NULLS FIRST` clauses. These match the live `.mv.db` exactly — which is the whole point of D024.

## Verification

Applied V1 to a fresh H2 via `org.h2.tools.RunScript` (exit 0), then re-dumped the resulting DB with `org.h2.tools.Script` and diffed against the live dump. After stripping runtime-only lines (CREATE USER, SET DB_CLOSE_DELAY, INSERTs, row-count comments) and normalizing whitespace, the two DDL statement sets are identical: 28 statements on each side, `diff` exits 0.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `java -cp h2-2.3.232.jar org.h2.tools.RunScript -url jdbc:h2:file:/tmp/v1test -user sa -script V1__baseline.sql` | 0 | pass | 1800ms |
| 2 | `diff <(normalize live-dump) <(normalize v1-applied) # DDL-only, whitespace-normalized` | 0 | pass | 50ms |

## Deviations

None.

## Known Issues

Server was stopped to release the H2 file lock; not restarted yet (T02 will wire Flyway and we'll start fresh). The `data/musicode-db.mv.db` file is intact and untouched by this task.

# T02: Flyway wired as single source of truth; ddl-auto flipped to validate in prod + test profiles

**Flyway wired as single source of truth; ddl-auto flipped to validate in prod + test profiles**

## What Happened

Added `org.flywaydb:flyway-core` to pom.xml (H2 dialect support lives in flyway-core itself, not a split module). Configured Flyway in `application.yml` (spring.flyway: enabled=true, locations=classpath:db/migration, baseline-on-migrate=true, baseline-version=0, validate-on-migrate=true) and mirrored the config in `application-test.yml` so the test profile migrates from the same V1 baseline. Flipped `spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto` from `update` to `validate` in both profiles — migrations are now the only path to schema change. Existing `.mv.db` files get `baseline-on-migrate` treatment so the first boot marks V1 as applied without re-running DDL. Full Maven verify went green with 187 tests and JaCoCo coverage above 80%.

tasks.title: Added AES-256-GCM TokenEncryptionService + v1:-prefixed EncryptedStringConverter with tolerant read for legacy plaintext.
tasks.one_liner: Added AES-256-GCM TokenEncryptionService + v1:-prefixed EncryptedStringConverter with tolerant read for legacy plaintext.
tasks.narrative:
Added `TokenEncryptionService` wrapping Spring Security's `Encryptors.delux` (AES-256-GCM + PBKDF2). Key is injected from `musicode.encryption.token-key` (env `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY`); constructor throws `IllegalStateException` if the key is blank so the app fails fast outside the test profile. The test profile sets a fixed key via `application-test.yml`.

`EncryptedStringConverter` is a JPA `AttributeConverter<String,String>`. Write path prepends a `v1:` version tag to the ciphertext; read path decrypts only `v1:`-prefixed values and passes anything else through untouched (tolerant read for pre-S03 plaintext). Because Hibernate instantiates converters outside the Spring context, a nested `@Component Initializer` pushes the `TokenEncryptionService` into a static `volatile` field via `@PostConstruct`. Converter throws if used before initialization — prevents silent failures.

Unit tests cover: round-trip, null handling, tolerant read for non-prefixed values, wrong-key decrypt failure, and the "service not initialized" guard on the converter. Added `application.yml` binding for `musicode.encryption.token-key: ${MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY:}`.
tasks.verification_result: `mvn verify` green: 187 tests / 0 failures / 0 errors / JaCoCo 86% instruction coverage (gate 80%). New unit tests (TokenEncryptionServiceTest, EncryptedStringConverterTest) pass. Startup against empty profile without key reproduces fail-fast IllegalStateException as designed.
key_decisions: Used Encryptors.delux (text-returning AES-GCM + PBKDF2) instead of Encryptors.stronger (bytes-returning) so converter can stay String->String.
Added 'v1:' version prefix on stored ciphertext to enable tolerant read of legacy plaintext and future key rotation.
Static volatile encryptionService reference on the converter + @PostConstruct Initializer — Hibernate builds converters outside Spring, and this avoids polluting entity classes with Spring wiring.

# T03: Added AES-256-GCM TokenEncryptionService + v1:-prefixed EncryptedStringConverter with tolerant read for legacy plaintext.

**Added AES-256-GCM TokenEncryptionService + v1:-prefixed EncryptedStringConverter with tolerant read for legacy plaintext.**

## What Happened

Added `TokenEncryptionService` wrapping Spring Security's `Encryptors.delux` (AES-256-GCM + PBKDF2). Key is injected from `musicode.encryption.token-key` (env `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY`); constructor throws `IllegalStateException` if the key is blank so the app fails fast outside the test profile. The test profile sets a fixed key via `application-test.yml`.

`EncryptedStringConverter` is a JPA `AttributeConverter<String,String>`. Write path prepends a `v1:` version tag to the ciphertext; read path decrypts only `v1:`-prefixed values and passes anything else through untouched (tolerant read for pre-S03 plaintext). Because Hibernate instantiates converters outside the Spring context, a nested `@Component Initializer` pushes the `TokenEncryptionService` into a static `volatile` field via `@PostConstruct`. Converter throws if used before initialization — prevents silent failures.

Unit tests cover: round-trip, null handling, tolerant read for non-prefixed values, wrong-key decrypt failure, and the "service not initialized" guard on the converter. Added `application.yml` binding for `musicode.encryption.token-key: ${MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY:}`.

## Verification

`mvn verify` green: 187 tests / 0 failures / 0 errors / JaCoCo 86% instruction coverage (gate 80%). New unit tests (TokenEncryptionServiceTest, EncryptedStringConverterTest) pass. Startup against empty profile without key reproduces fail-fast IllegalStateException as designed.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `mvn -f musicode-server/pom.xml verify` | 0 | pass | 180000ms |

## Deviations

None.

## Known Issues

None.

- `musicode-server/src/main/java/com/musicode/service/TokenEncryptionService.java`
- `musicode-server/src/main/java/com/musicode/util/EncryptedStringConverter.java`
- `musicode-server/src/test/java/com/musicode/service/TokenEncryptionServiceTest.java`
- `musicode-server/src/test/java/com/musicode/util/EncryptedStringConverterTest.java`
- `musicode-server/src/main/resources/application.yml`
- `musicode-server/src/test/resources/application-test.yml`
tasks.description: Add MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY env var (fail-fast at startup if missing outside test profile). Implement TokenEncryptionService using Spring Security's Encryptors.stronger (AES-GCM, PBKDF2). Implement EncryptedStringConverter (JPA AttributeConverter<String,String>): on write wraps ciphertext with a 'v1:' prefix; on read, values starting with 'v1:' are decrypted, anything else is returned as-is (tolerant read for pre-migration rows). Unit tests cover: round-trip, plaintext tolerance, null handling, wrong-key failure.

tasks.title: Annotated User scrobble tokens with @Convert, added idempotent TokenMigrationRunner, and documented the encryption key.
tasks.one_liner: Annotated User scrobble tokens with @Convert, added idempotent TokenMigrationRunner, and documented the encryption key.
tasks.narrative:
Annotated `User.lastfmSessionKey` and `User.listenbrainzToken` with `@Convert(converter = EncryptedStringConverter.class)` so JPA transparently encrypts writes and decrypts reads.

Implemented `TokenMigrationRunner` as an `ApplicationRunner`. At boot it queries the `users` table via raw `JdbcTemplate` for rows in `lastfm_session_key` / `listenbrainz_token` that do **not** start with the `v1:` prefix, encrypts them through `TokenEncryptionService`, and writes the prefixed ciphertext back with a parameterized UPDATE. Column names come from a fixed `List.of(...)` constant — safe to concatenate into SQL. The runner is idempotent: a second boot finds zero legacy rows and exits silently at DEBUG. Logs only aggregate counts — no token material.

Unit test (`TokenMigrationRunnerTest`) uses Mockito to verify: plaintext rows are updated with `v1:`-prefixed ciphertext, already-encrypted rows are skipped, empty result short-circuits without writes.

Updated `.env.example` and `SCROBBLING.md` with `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` — generation command (`openssl rand -hex 32`), mandatory nature outside test profile, and a note that rotation requires re-encrypting all `v1:` rows (future work, BSM called out as an option only).
tasks.verification_result: `mvn verify` green: 187 tests / 0 failures / 0 errors / JaCoCo 86% instruction coverage (≥80% gate). New TokenMigrationRunnerTest passes. Integration path covered by existing scrobble flow tests — User entity now round-trips tokens through the converter without regressions.
key_decisions: Used raw JdbcTemplate (not JPA) in the migration runner so the WHERE filter on the stored (encrypted) column works against the literal bytes — JPA would decrypt on read and break the predicate.
Runner is ApplicationRunner rather than @PostConstruct on the service: guarantees it fires after Flyway and the full context is ready.
Hardcoded column list as a constant (no reflection / metamodel lookup) — only two columns, explicitness wins.
