# T04: Annotated User scrobble tokens with @Convert, added idempotent TokenMigrationRunner, and documented the encryption key.

**Annotated User scrobble tokens with @Convert, added idempotent TokenMigrationRunner, and documented the encryption key.**

## What Happened

Annotated `User.lastfmSessionKey` and `User.listenbrainzToken` with `@Convert(converter = EncryptedStringConverter.class)` so JPA transparently encrypts writes and decrypts reads.

Implemented `TokenMigrationRunner` as an `ApplicationRunner`. At boot it queries the `users` table via raw `JdbcTemplate` for rows in `lastfm_session_key` / `listenbrainz_token` that do **not** start with the `v1:` prefix, encrypts them through `TokenEncryptionService`, and writes the prefixed ciphertext back with a parameterized UPDATE. Column names come from a fixed `List.of(...)` constant — safe to concatenate into SQL. The runner is idempotent: a second boot finds zero legacy rows and exits silently at DEBUG. Logs only aggregate counts — no token material.

Unit test (`TokenMigrationRunnerTest`) uses Mockito to verify: plaintext rows are updated with `v1:`-prefixed ciphertext, already-encrypted rows are skipped, empty result short-circuits without writes.

Updated `.env.example` and `SCROBBLING.md` with `MUSICODE_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY` — generation command (`openssl rand -hex 32`), mandatory nature outside test profile, and a note that rotation requires re-encrypting all `v1:` rows (future work, BSM called out as an option only).

## Verification

`mvn verify` green: 187 tests / 0 failures / 0 errors / JaCoCo 86% instruction coverage (≥80% gate). New TokenMigrationRunnerTest passes. Integration path covered by existing scrobble flow tests — User entity now round-trips tokens through the converter without regressions.

**Created centralized audioGraph.ts module encapsulating the entire Web Audio API pipeline**

## What Happened

Created `musicode-ui/src/audio/audioGraph.ts` — a module-level singleton that owns the complete audio graph: `HTMLAudioElement → MediaElementSourceNode → GainNode → [insert chain] → AnalyserNode → destination`. The module exposes a clean API surface: `init()` (lazy AudioContext creation on user gesture), `setSource/play/pause/stop/seek`, `setVolume` (via GainNode, element stays at 1.0), `getAnalyser()` for visualizer, and callback registration for timeupdate/ended/loadedmetadata. # T01: Refactored audioGraph to dual-element architecture with A/B HTMLAudioElements sharing a single gain→analyser→destination chain

**Refactored audioGraph to dual-element architecture with A/B HTMLAudioElements sharing a single gain→analyser→destination chain**

## What Happened

Rewrote audioGraph.ts to manage two HTMLAudioElements (elementA, elementB) with independent MediaElementSourceNodes. Both sources connect to the shared gainNode→analyserNode→destination chain. Added prepareNext(src) to pre-load on the inactive element, swap() to switch active element, and stop()/destroy() to clean up both. The active element is tracked via an activeSlot variable. connect/disconnect helpers manage source node lifecycle. Public API remains backward-compatible — play(), pause(), setVolume(), setMuted() operate on the active element transparently.

## Verification

TypeScript compiles clean. Manual browser test: play a track, pre-load triggers on inactive element, no audio glitch on active track during pre-load.

## Verification Evidence

Added gapless transition logic to usePlayer.ts: 1) On timeupdate, when remaining time ≤3s, calls audioGraph.prepareNext() with the next track URL based on queue/shuffle/repeat state. 2) On ended event, calls audioGraph.swap() then dispatches NEXT to update React state, with a flag indicating gapless swap so loadTrack skips redundant loading. 3) Edge cases handled: repeat-one restarts same track without swap, repeat-all wraps to queue[0] with gapless pre-load, repeat-off at queue end stops playback. 4) Manual skip (next/prev) cancels pending pre-load via audioGraph.cancelPreload() and loads target track directly. Uses preloadedRef and preloadingRef to avoid duplicate pre-loads.

Restructured audioGraph.ts from a single MediaElementSource to a dual-slot architecture (sourceA/sourceB) each with independent GainNode. Signal chain: sourceA→gainA→masterGain→analyser→destination, sourceB→gainB→masterGain→analyser→destination. Added prepareCrossfade() to pre-load the next track into the inactive slot. The activeSlot flip mechanism enables seamless crossfade transitions. Backward-compatible — gapless playback continues to work when crossfade is 0.

tasks.description: Restructure the audio graph from single-gain to dual-gain topology. Current: sourceA/B → gainNode → analyser → dest. # T02: Implemented crossfade logic with Web Audio gain ramps and preference persistence

**Implemented crossfade logic with Web Audio gain ramps and preference persistence**

## What Happened

Added startCrossfade() using linearRampToValueAtTime for smooth gain transitions. The active source ramps from 1→0 while the prepared source ramps from 0→1 over the configured duration. Added crossfadeDuration to audioPreferences (localStorage persistence). Timeupdate handler triggers crossfade when currentTime > duration - crossfadeDuration. Guard prevents crossfade on short tracks (duration must exceed cfDuration + 1). cancelCrossfade() resets gains and clears timers for skip/stop scenarios. Fixed critical bug where dispatch('NEXT') triggered setSource which cancelled the crossfade — added isCrossfading() guard and immediate activeSlot flip.

## Verification

Build passes. Manual listening test confirms gradual volume ramp between tracks. Skip during crossfade correctly cancels and plays next track.

tasks.description: Add crossfade transition logic to audioGraph.ts: when crossfadeDuration > 0, instead of instant swap, both elements play simultaneously during the crossfade window. Active element's gain ramps from 1→0, next element's gain ramps from 0→1, both using linearRampToValueAtTime. Add crossfadeDuration to AudioPreferences (default 0 = gapless). Add crossfade slider UI to preferences/settings. Modify usePlayer.ts onEnded handler: check crossfade preference, if > 0 call new crossfade method instead of swap(). Pre-load threshold should be max(PRELOAD_THRESHOLD, crossfadeDuration + 1) to ensure next track is loaded before crossfade starts. In timeupdate handler: when remaining <= crossfadeDuration, start the crossfade (don't wait for ended event).

# T03: Smooth fade-out animation when visualizer playback stops or pauses

**Smooth fade-out animation when visualizer playback stops or pauses**

## What Happened

Added decayRef tracking (1.0 while playing, decrements to 0 on pause/stop). Each frame during decay overlays rgba(0,0,0,0.1) — visualizer fades in ~10 frames (~170ms at 60fps). On full decay, resets waveform smoothing buffer. Works identically for all 3 modes. Committed as 4cdca62.

# T03: Added CurrentTrackContext for narrow subscription — TrackList no longer re-renders on currentTime ticks

**Added CurrentTrackContext for narrow subscription — TrackList no longer re-renders on currentTime ticks**

## What Happened

PlayerStateContext carries all state including currentTime (~4Hz updates). Components like TrackList and Visualizer only need trackId and isPlaying. Added a dedicated CurrentTrackContext with a useMemo'd value that only changes when currentTrack.id or isPlaying changes. Migrated TrackList, AlbumDetailPage, and Visualizer from usePlayerState to useCurrentTrackInfo. This eliminates ~4 re-renders/second for all track list and visualizer components.

## Verification

TypeScript compiles cleanly. Hooks ordering fixed (useMemo/useCallback moved above early returns).

# T01: Replaced div-based sliders with native range inputs in ProgressBar and VolumeControl

**Replaced div-based sliders with native range inputs in ProgressBar and VolumeControl**

## What Happened

Both ProgressBar and VolumeControl used div+onClick handlers for seek/volume — not focusable, not keyboard accessible. Replaced with native `<input type="range">` elements. ProgressBar gets aria-label="Seek position" with aria-valuetext showing current/total time. VolumeControl gets aria-label="Volume" with percentage valuetext. Mute button gets dynamic aria-label reflecting muted/unmuted state. Both sliders styled with CSS pseudo-elements to match the dark theme (custom thumb, track gradient fill). Focus-visible rings added for keyboard navigation feedback.

# T02: Smart retry in ScrobbleService — skip auth/config errors, retry only timeout/server errors

**Smart retry in ScrobbleService — skip auth/config errors, retry only timeout/server errors**

## What Happened

Exceptions still retry (defensive). Updated ScrobbleServiceTest with new scenarios: authError_noRetry, configError_noRetry, retryableError_retriesWithBackoff. All 15 ScrobbleService tests pass.

# T04: Refactored usePlayer to 190 LOC orchestrator composing 3 extracted hooks

**Refactored usePlayer to 190 LOC orchestrator composing 3 extracted hooks**

## What Happened

Rewrote usePlayer as a thin orchestrator that composes useScrobble, useMediaSession, and useGapless. Changed ownership tracking from useRef to useState so child hooks re-render when ownership is claimed (fixing a timing bug where isOwner.current was read at render time before the effect set it). Public API unchanged — all 5 consumers (AppShell, PlayerBar, SearchPage, AlbumDetailPage, TracksPage) needed zero changes. 418 LOC → 190 LOC (55% reduction). Slightly over the 150 target because the public API callbacks (~50 LOC) are irreducible.

## Verification

vitest --run: 109/109 pass. tsc --noEmit: clean. wc -l usePlayer.ts: 190. No import changes in any consumer file.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `npx vitest --run` | 0 | pass | 3000ms |
| 2 | `npx tsc --noEmit` | 0 | pass | 5000ms |

## Deviations

usePlayer.ts is 190 LOC instead of target 150. The ~40 LOC over target is the public API surface (playTrack, playAlbum, pause, resume, next, prev, seek, setVolume, toggleShuffle, toggleRepeat) which cannot be extracted without changing the hook's contract.

## Known Issues

None.

- `musicode-ui/src/hooks/useScrobble.ts`
- `musicode-ui/src/hooks/useMediaSession.ts`

# T03: useDynamicTheme hook: opt-in color extraction with CSS variable injection

**useDynamicTheme hook: opt-in color extraction with CSS variable injection**

## What Happened

Created useDynamicTheme hook that reads dynamicTheme pref from localStorage, subscribes to currentTrack via PlayerContext, extracts colors when enabled and track changes, sets --np-color-1/--np-color-2/--np-bg CSS variables on document root. Toggle persists preference. Clears variables when disabled or on unmount. Uses albumIdRef to skip redundant extractions. Checks cache synchronously before async extraction for instant palette on revisited albums.

# T02: Artwork crossfade with dual-image approach on track change

**Artwork crossfade with dual-image approach on track change**

## What Happened

Implemented artwork crossfade using dual-image technique: when track changes, the previous cover stays visible with a fade-out animation (np-cover-exit, 0.5s) while the new cover fades in (np-cover-enter, 0.5s). Previous cover src stored in state, cleared after animation completes. CSS keyframes handle the opacity transitions.

tasks.title: WaveformService extracts peaks via ffmpeg ProcessBuilder with JSON file cache
tasks.one_liner: WaveformService extracts peaks via ffmpeg ProcessBuilder with JSON file cache
tasks.narrative: Created WaveformService that runs ffmpeg via ProcessBuilder to extract raw PCM samples from audio files, downsamples to 200 normalized peaks (0.0-1.0), and caches results as JSON in data/waveforms/{trackId}.json. Uses @Value-injected paths for ffmpeg binary and waveforms directory. Handles cache hits, missing files, and ffmpeg errors gracefully. Fixed a critical deadlock bug where ffmpeg stderr buffer filled up and blocked the process — resolved by using redirectErrorStream(true) and -v error flag.
tasks.verification_result: mvn test -Dtest=WaveformServiceTest — 7 tests pass
key_decisions: Used redirectErrorStream(true) + -v error to prevent ffmpeg stderr deadlock
200 peaks as default resolution — good balance of detail vs payload size
JSON file cache per trackId — simple, no DB dependency

# T01: WaveformService extracts peaks via ffmpeg ProcessBuilder with JSON file cache

**WaveformService extracts peaks via ffmpeg ProcessBuilder with JSON file cache**

## What Happened

Created WaveformService that runs ffmpeg via ProcessBuilder to extract raw PCM samples from audio files, downsamples to 200 normalized peaks (0.0-1.0), and caches results as JSON in data/waveforms/{trackId}.json. Uses @Value-injected paths for ffmpeg binary and waveforms directory. Handles cache hits, missing files, and ffmpeg errors gracefully. tasks.title: Reel physics and tape winding animation
tasks.description: Implement reel animation with accurate tape physics. Each reel has a hub radius and tape radius that changes with progress (left shrinks, right grows). Angular velocity = linearSpeed / currentRadius (constant linear tape speed). Tape is visible as brown area on each reel (hub to tape radius). Use requestAnimationFrame loop. Props: progress (0-1), isPlaying (drives spin), onFrame callback for parent sync. When paused, reels decelerate with inertia wobble. When seeking, reels spin at accelerated speed in the correct direction.

# T01: Created ThemeProvider context, types, useTheme hook, and token sets for all 3 themes (Evolved/Novatouch/Minimal)

**Created ThemeProvider context, types, useTheme hook, and token sets for all 3 themes (Evolved/Novatouch/Minimal)**

## What Happened

Built the complete theme architecture foundation:

1. Created `src/themes/types.ts` with ThemeName, ShellLayout, ThemeTokens (40+ tokens), ThemeConfig, and ThemeContextValue types.
2. Created token sets for all 3 themes: evolved (zinc+indigo, sidebar-expanded), novatouch (cool violet, sidebar-icons), minimal (soft purple, horizontal nav, mono typography).
3. Created ThemeProvider that converts camelCase token keys to CSS custom properties (--mc-*), applies them to :root, persists selection to localStorage, and sets data-theme attribute.
4. Created useTheme hook with proper context guard.
5. Integrated ThemeProvider into App.tsx wrapping the entire app tree (outside AuthProvider/PlayerProvider so themes work even on login page).
6. Barrel export from src/themes/index.ts.

# T04: Built ThemeSelector component and wired end-to-end theme switching across all 3 shells, settled in Settings page

**Built ThemeSelector component and wired end-to-end theme switching across all 3 shells, settled in Settings page**

## What Happened

Created ThemeSelector as a segmented control with Evolved/Novatouch/Minimal buttons. Initially placed in TopBar (Evolved), MinimalShell header, and as a popover in NovatouchShell sidebar. After user testing, iterated on placement: unified accent colors to indigo across all themes, repositioned the Novatouch popover. Ultimately moved the theme selector exclusively to the Settings page (Appearance section) per user feedback — this is where music apps typically place theme controls. Removed all inline/popover theme selectors from shells. End-to-end flow works: clicking a theme in Settings updates ThemeContext → CSS variables swap on :root → layout shell switches → choice persists in localStorage across reloads.

## Verification

npm run build passes. Verified in browser: all 3 themes switch correctly from Settings, accent colors are consistent indigo, layout shells swap, localStorage persists choice across reload. User confirmed all themes working correctly.

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `npx tsc --noEmit` | 0 | pass | 8000ms |
| 2 | `npx vite build` | 0 | pass | 12000ms |

## Deviations

Plan specified TopBar placement. After iterating with user feedback (popover too intrusive in Novatouch, accent colors inconsistent), settled on Settings page as the single location for theme selection.

## Known Issues

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- `musicode-ui/src/components/common/ThemeSelector.tsx`

- `musicode-ui/src/components/layout/shells/NovatouchShell.tsx`
- `musicode-ui/src/components/layout/shells/MinimalShell.tsx`
- `musicode-ui/src/themes/tokens/novatouch.ts`
- `musicode-ui/src/themes/tokens/minimal.ts`

decisions.choice: HTML5 Audio element with HTTP Range streaming (206 Partial Content)
decisions.rationale: FLAC is natively supported in Chrome, Edge, and Firefox. No transcoding needed for desktop use. Range headers enable seeking in large files (30-80MB FLACs) without full download.

decisions.choice: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + TanStack Query
decisions.rationale: Modern, fast dev experience. Vite for instant HMR. TypeScript for type safety. Tailwind for rapid styling. TanStack Query for server state management with caching and pagination. User's preferred stack per PLAN.md.

decisions.choice: React 19 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + TanStack Query
decisions.rationale: Modern, fast dev experience. Vite for instant HMR. TypeScript for type safety. Tailwind for rapid styling. TanStack Query for server state. User's preferred stack.

decisions.choice: Spring Boot 3 + Java 21 + Gradle (Kotlin DSL)
decisions.rationale: User's habitual stack. Modern Java features, virtual threads potential, native compilation path. Gradle Kotlin DSL for type-safe build config.

decisions.choice: H2 in file mode (embedded)
decisions.rationale: Zero config, ships as JAR dependency, no external server. Sufficient for single-user personal media library.

decisions.choice: JAudioTagger 3.x
decisions.rationale: Pure Java, supports ID3/Vorbis/MP4 tags and embedded cover art under a unified API. Standard in the Java audio ecosystem.

decisions.choice: HTML5 Audio element + HTTP Range headers (206 Partial Content)
decisions.rationale: FLAC natively supported in Chrome/Edge/Firefox. Range headers enable seeking in large files without full download. No transcoding needed for desktop.

decisions.choice: Maven (supersedes D004 which said Gradle)
decisions.rationale: Maven es el stack habitual del usuario. En un proyecto donde el frontend ya es aprendizaje nuevo (React), mantener el backend en terreno conocido reduce la carga cognitiva. Gradle Kotlin DSL tiene curva de aprendizaje innecesaria aquí.

decisions.decision: Audio format support scope for MVP
decisions.choice: Solo FLAC en MVP, multi-formato diferido
decisions.rationale: Reducir alcance del MVP a un solo formato simplifica el desarrollo. FLAC es lossless y el caso de uso principal del usuario. MP3/OGG/M4A se añaden después del MVP.

decisions.choice: Windows 11 como entorno de desarrollo y ejecución principal
decisions.rationale: El usuario desarrolla y usará Musicode en Windows 11. Afecta rutas de filesystem (backslash, letras de unidad), volume mounts en Docker Desktop (WSL2), y testing. El backend debe usar java.nio.file.Path para abstracción de rutas cross-platform.

decisions.choice: Keep current dark theme (zinc + indigo accents). No light mode. Custom themes deferred to future milestone.
decisions.rationale: User likes the current dark palette (zinc grays + indigo blues). Light mode not needed. Custom themes are a future possibility but not priority.

decisions.decision: Minimum test coverage threshold for the project
decisions.choice: 80% line coverage enforced via JaCoCo (backend) and @vitest/coverage-v8 (frontend). Builds fail if coverage drops below threshold.
decisions.rationale: Ensures all new code ships with meaningful test coverage. 80% is pragmatic — catches gaps without forcing pointless tests on trivial code.

decisions.choice: Stay with H2 — no migration needed currently
decisions.rationale: H2 is giving zero problems. SQLite is natural next step if needed. PostgreSQL only if full-text search or complex analytics (listening stats) are required in the future.
decisions.revisable: Yes — revisit if H2 hits limitations or advanced features are needed

decisions.choice: Deferred — token blacklist + rate limiting + session cache in future milestone
decisions.rationale: M003 uses H2 table for refresh tokens. Redis milestone will compare approaches and teach Spring Data Redis, TTL, pub/sub for invalidation.

decisions.decision: CSRF protection strategy for cookie-based JWT auth
decisions.choice: CSRF disabled, rely on SameSite=Strict cookies
decisions.rationale: SameSite=Strict prevents the browser from sending cookies on cross-origin requests, which is the primary CSRF vector. Our SPA is same-origin. If we ever relax to SameSite=Lax (for external links), we'll need to add CookieCsrfTokenRepository.
decisions.revisable: Yes — revisit if SameSite is relaxed to Lax

decisions.decision: DTOs as Records vs Lombok classes
decisions.choice: Migrate DTOs to Java Records in cleanup task post-M003
decisions.rationale: Java 21 Records are idiomatic for immutable data carriers. All current DTOs qualify. JPA entities stay with Lombok. Deferred to avoid churn mid-milestone.

decisions.decision: Post-M006 milestone sequence and feature grouping
decisions.choice: M007 Listening Intelligence → M008 Audio Experience → M009 Smart Library → M010 Visual Experience → M011 Integrations & Streaming
decisions.rationale: M007 first: redondest milestone (backend + frontend + external API), each slice delivers visible value. M008 second: touches the core player, defines identity as an audiophile tool. M009-M011 follow by decreasing priority — smart library management, then visual polish, then external integrations. Letras (.lrc) considered for M008 but kept in M011 to avoid scope creep on the audio pipeline milestone.
decisions.revisable: Yes — features can move between milestones during planning

decisions.choice: SpringDoc OpenAPI 2.8.14 with springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui
decisions.rationale: Auto-generated from controller annotations, Swagger UI for interactive testing, OpenAPI 3.0 spec reusable. Chosen over Bruno (manual maintenance). Pinned to 2.8.14 — 2.8.15+ incompatible with Spring Boot 3.4.4.

decisions.choice: Playwright with Chromium, sequential single-worker, HTML reporter
decisions.rationale: Real browser testing covers integration gaps unit tests miss. Sequential + single worker avoids H2 race conditions. Vite dev server auto-started via webServer config. Backend must be pre-started manually.

decisions.choice: Per-user scrobbling via Last.fm session key + ListenBrainz token, async fire-and-forget from PlayController
decisions.rationale: Two-level credentials: app-level API key for Last.fm (env vars), user-level tokens stored in User entity. Async with retry — never blocks playback. Last.fm mobile auth flow gives permanent session key. ListenBrainz uses simple bearer token. Both optional, configured per user in Settings.

decisions.decision: Album grouping strategy during library scan
decisions.choice: ALBUM_ARTIST tag for album grouping, fallback to track ARTIST
decisions.rationale: Prevents compilation albums from fragmenting into N entries when each track has a different artist. ALBUM_ARTIST is the ID3/Vorbis standard for this purpose. Compilations without ALBUM_ARTIST still fragment — fixable via future metadata editing feature.

decisions.choice: Recharts for stats charts in the frontend
decisions.rationale: Recharts is React-native, composable, well-maintained, and matches the project's dark theme with custom styling. Simpler than D3 for the bar/line charts needed. Only used in StatsPage.

decisions.decision: Pause new features and consolidate existing codebase first
decisions.choice: Consolidation milestone (M008) before new features. Address test gaps, verify scrobbling, add Flyway, lazy routes, and frontend structure.
decisions.rationale: 7 milestones of feature work accumulated tech debt: untested services, unverified external integrations, no DB migrations, growing frontend without structure. Shipping more features on a shaky foundation multiplies future cost. Original M008 (Audio Experience) renumbered to M009+.

decisions.decision: Flyway baseline strategy for existing H2 database
decisions.choice: Export real H2 schema via SCRIPT TO before creating V1__baseline.sql — never reconstruct from JPA entities
decisions.rationale: ddl-auto:update generates schema incrementally over 7 milestones. The actual H2 schema may differ from what JPA entities would generate fresh (column ordering, constraint names, index quirks). Using the real schema dump as V1 prevents subtle mismatches that cause Flyway validation failures.

decisions.decision: Split original M009 Audio Experience into two milestones
decisions.choice: M009: Audio Pipeline & Gapless (foundational graph + gapless) → M010: Audio Experience (crossfade, EQ, enhanced visualizer). Supersedes D017 milestone sequence.
decisions.rationale: Pipeline migration and gapless are architecturally foundational — they change how audio flows. If they fail, everything built on top (crossfade, EQ, visualizer) collapses. Better to validate the base as a closed milestone before extending it. Remaining milestones shift: M010→M011 Smart Library, M011→M012 Visual Experience, M012→M013 Integrations.

decisions.choice: Centralized audioGraph.ts module encapsulating the full Web Audio API graph: source → GainNode → [insert chain] → AnalyserNode → destination. Hooks consume this module instead of managing their own singletons.
decisions.rationale: Current code has dispersed singletons (globalAudio in usePlayer.ts, audioContext/analyserNode in useAudioAnalyser.ts) that don't coordinate. A single module gives one point for inserting/removing nodes (EQ, crossfade, effects). Extending the current separation would require coordinating between unrelated modules.

decisions.choice: Dual HTMLAudioElement with precise swap — two audio elements, each with its own createMediaElementSource connected to the graph. Pre-load next track on second element, swap at track end.
decisions.rationale: Simpler than buffer puente (partial fetch + decodeAudioData). No memory problem with large FLACs (30-80MB → 200-500MB decoded in RAM). Near-gapless timing (~0-50ms) sufficient for personal use. Alternative: full AudioBuffer decode is impractical at ~101MB per 5-minute stereo 44.1kHz track.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if near-gapless proves insufficient for live albums

decisions.choice: localStorage for all audio preferences (volume, shuffle, repeat, future EQ/crossfade settings)
decisions.rationale: Device-local, simple, no backend changes needed. For a personal project, cross-device sync is not required. If needed later, migrate to User entity backend persistence.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if cross-device sync becomes desired

decisions.choice: Gapless always on. Crossfade opt-in (slider 0-12s, default 0/off). EQ opt-in (default flat/off). Visualizer modes selectable (current bars as default).
decisions.rationale: Philosophy: "tu audio suena exactamente como antes hasta que tú decidas cambiarlo." Gapless is objectively better with no downside. Crossfade and EQ are subjective preferences — activating them by default alters audio without consent, which is anti-audiophile.

decisions.choice: 5-band parametric EQ: 60Hz (lowshelf), 230Hz (peaking), 910Hz (peaking), 3.6kHz (peaking), 14kHz (highshelf). BiquadFilterNode instances in series.
decisions.rationale: 5 bands give sufficient control without overloading the UI. Logarithmic distribution covers the audible spectrum evenly. 3 bands too basic, 10 bands excessive for personal use. Standard distribution used by many consumer EQs.
decisions.revisable: Yes — band count can be adjusted

decisions.decision: Crossfade graph topology — per-source gain nodes
decisions.choice: sourceA → gainA, sourceB → gainB, both merge into masterGain → analyserNode → destination. Individual gainA/gainB handle crossfade ramps (linearRampToValueAtTime). masterGain handles user volume.
decisions.rationale: Crossfade needs independent volume control per source for fade-in/fade-out overlap. The existing single gainNode can't fade two sources independently. Per-source gains before the master gain preserve the existing volume/mute behavior untouched.

decisions.decision: EQ node insertion point in audio graph
decisions.choice: 5 BiquadFilterNodes in series between masterGain and analyserNode: masterGain → eq1 → eq2 → eq3 → eq4 → eq5 → analyserNode → destination. When EQ disabled, bypass all 5 and connect masterGain directly to analyser.
decisions.rationale: EQ must apply to the mixed signal (after both sources merge at masterGain), before the analyser (so visualizer reflects EQ'd audio). Bypass via disconnect/reconnect avoids processing overhead when flat.

decisions.decision: Crossfade and gapless are mutually exclusive at runtime
decisions.choice: When crossfadeDuration > 0: use fade overlap (gainA ramps down, gainB ramps up, both play simultaneously during crossfade window). When crossfadeDuration === 0: use gapless swap from M009 (instant element swap on ended event). Decision made per-transition based on localStorage preference.
decisions.rationale: Gapless seeks zero-gap without overlap; crossfade seeks overlap. Combining them is contradictory. Runtime check on the preference value is the simplest correct implementation.

decisions.choice: CSS grid-template-rows transition (0fr → 1fr) with opacity fade. No animation library dependency.
decisions.rationale: Native CSS, GPU-friendly, works with dynamic content height. Framer Motion (~30KB) unjustified for a single expand/collapse. Can layer opacity transition for polish.

decisions.decision: Visualizer expandable panel scope for M010
decisions.choice: Option A: compact strip (current 48px) + click to expand to larger panel over player bar. No full-screen mode in M010.
decisions.rationale: Full-screen is scope creep. The expand panel covers the UX need. Full-screen can be trivially added later if desired.

decisions.choice: Discarded — not implemented
decisions.rationale: Filesystem watcher (inotify/WatchService) adds complexity for marginal value in a personal single-user app. Manual rescan from the UI is sufficient. The milestone slot was skipped; numbering jumps from M014 to M016 intentionally.
decisions.revisable: Yes — if automatic library updates become a pain point

decisions.when_context: 2026-05-02, after evaluating Astra Music and 4 strategic options
decisions.choice: VPS deployment with URL access — no Electron/Tauri/exe packaging
decisions.rationale: David will control the server and create user accounts as admin. Users access Musicode via browser URL. This eliminates distribution friction without abandoning the web architecture (multi-user, Docker Compose, Caddy reverse proxy). Evaluated alternatives: Electron wrapper (heavy, loses multi-device), Astra fork (foreign codebase, single-user architecture), new Tauri project (months of rewrite). The web stack is the right choice when the server is centrally hosted.

decisions.when_context: 2026-05-02, after creating and reviewing 3 interactive mockups
decisions.decision: UX overhaul approach — three selectable layout themes
decisions.choice: Three themes with structurally different layouts: Novatouch (icon sidebar, Astra-inspired), Evolved (labeled sidebar, Musicode identity + Astra polish), Minimal (horizontal nav, Spotify-style). ThemeProvider with interchangeable layout components, not just CSS variables.
decisions.rationale: Each theme has a fundamentally different navigation structure (56px icon sidebar vs expanded sidebar with labels vs no sidebar). CSS-only theming cannot achieve this — layout components must be swappable. Inspired by Astra Music analysis but maintaining Musicode identity. Starting with Evolved as the base theme since it preserves existing functionality while adding polish.
decisions.revisable: Yes — theme count and specifics may change

decisions.when_context: 2026-05-02, David discovered and installed Astra, compared with Musicode
decisions.decision: UX reference project for Musicode polish
decisions.choice: Astra Music (astramusic.dev) as design reference — cloned to reference/astra-music/, screenshots in reference/screenshots/, but no code reuse or fork
decisions.rationale: Astra has superior visual polish (glassmorphism, hover effects, play bar layout, data density) but is architecturally incompatible (Electron single-user vs web multi-user).
